old_claim.note: Measuring Rods & Lines provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct Measuring Rods & Lines to stone_paved_courtyards edge remains because the cited Gobekli Tepe evidence supports megalithic enclosure construction but does not establish standardized rods or marked lines as a prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: UNESCO describes monolithic T-shaped pillars carved from the adjacent limestone plateau and semi-subterranean architecture, but does not cite standardized rods, ropes, or marked measuring lines.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports shaped-stone architectural construction at Gobekli Tepe, not a measurement-tool dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the source documents limestone carving and enclosure architecture without measurement-tool evidence, the measuring-rods edge overstates the supported prerequisite set.

ontology_before: The node depended on measuring rods and lines, overconstraining a source-backed megalithic enclosure claim with an unsupported measurement-infrastructure dependency.
ontology_after: The node keeps masonry as the architectural enabler while leaving measuring rods and lines to nodes where direct surveying evidence is available.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: removed the measuring_rods_and_lines prerequisite. Preserved: masonry remains the source-backed enabling edge.
would_reject_if: A source shows standardized rods or marked measuring lines were required for the cited Gobekli Tepe enclosures.
The node is narrowed to formal surveyed courtyard paving rather than megalithic enclosures.
A separate surveyed_stone_courtyards node is introduced and intentionally depends on measuring_rods_and_lines.
short_reason: The cited enclosure source does not support measuring rods and lines as a prerequisite.
